commitMessage:
Bug 578491 - Switch JRE to another JRE results in re-ordering

This change preserves classpath order on JRE version changes during
BuildPathBasePage.moveCPElementAcrossNode(). In addition, a small
optimization is added to the first loop of moveCPElementAcrossNode() -
the loop is exited when the root element of the selected element is
found.

As is, for a move from modulepath to classpath (e.g. change JRE 11 to
JRE 8), the method moves children of the root container for modulepath
to classpath. Additionally, it removes the current JRE container, and
adds the new JRE container. This breaks classpath order in two ways.

First, the order of children in the root container is changed, due to
the removal and addition of the JRE container (if it was at first
position, now its at last position).

And second, LibrariesWorkbookPage.updateClasspathList() is unable to
preserve order of the classpath due to first being invoked with no
classpath containers in any root container. To then be invoked with the
correct elements in the root containers. The method loses track of the
correct positioning of the classpath containers.

The above is true also when moving from classpath to modulepath (e.g.
change JRE 8 to JRE 11).

Change-Id: I2ac18860deb420da611bf3525eead6b3ae683e24
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190227
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Fixes an internal bug in an Eclipse JDT UI component.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for local variable introduction, complex control flow modification (if/else, break), and manipulating list elements at specific indices (which is currently not supported for mutable lists like `getChildren()`).
summary: This commit fixes a UI re-ordering bug in JDT's BuildPathBasePage by preserving the classpath order when moving JRE elements. It involves introducing local variables, modifying control flow with `break` statements, using `List.indexOf()` and `List.add(index, element)` to reinsert elements at their original positions, and adding conditional logic for firing change events. These changes are highly specific to the internal UI logic and involve complex structural modifications that are not expressible by the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 450663 - don't fire UI label updates after decorator disposal

We should not fire decorator updates in case the enclosing view/editor
is closed.

Change-Id: I759c1f20316cc4b3ca729dfc8bf6c7f1f42134f0
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190514
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Andrey Loskutov <loskutov@gmx.de>
categoryReason: Fixes an internal bug in an Eclipse JDT UI component.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires a mechanism to bind method parameters (e.g., `IProgressMonitor monitor` from `Job.run`) into placeholders for use in the replacement pattern. The current DSL can identify the method context but not extract its parameters as bindable placeholders.
summary: This commit fixes Bug 450663 by preventing UI label updates after decorator disposal. It modifies a boolean condition in `ProblemsLabelDecorator.java` from `changes.length > 0` to `changes.length > 0 && !monitor.isCanceled()`. This change is a direct expression modification. However, the `monitor` variable is a method parameter which the current DSL cannot directly bind for use in the replacement. The addition of `fListeners.clear()` within a new `if` block in `dispose()` is a structural change not directly supported by the DSL. Therefore, this pattern is classified as YELLOW.
dslValidationResult: Parse error at line 1: Rule starting at line 1 is missing ';;' terminator (line 1)

commitMessage:
Bug 577919 - Enable callees based on implementors

Enable callees based on implementors search for JDT.UI by letting the
configurability to JDT.LS.

This fix also improve the callee hierarchy by adding support for showing
multiple implementations and expand on those. Also this fix add support
for triggering call hierarchies on method declarations on interface
methods and abstract methods.

Change-Id: I5eb1a309fdce1d93ff5486fce6bad046d5d091c1
Signed-off-by: Gayan Perera <gayanper@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/189117
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: Introduces new UI features and corresponding logic, involving structural code additions not suitable for pattern-based DSL rules.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for inserting new control flow blocks (`if` statement), instantiating and configuring UI widgets, and declaring new constants/fields. The current DSL is primarily for expression/statement replacement.
summary: This commit introduces new functionality to the JDT Call Hierarchy view, enabling the display of callees based on implementors. It involves adding a new conditional block to `CallHierarchyLabelProvider.java` to format 'potential callee' labels, as well as introducing new NLS messages and UI preference controls in `JavaBasePreferencePage.java`. These changes primarily consist of structural code additions (new `if` blocks, new declarations, UI component instantiation/configuration) which are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations.

commitMessage:
Bug 575330 - Provide support for Refactor > Rename of Modules in the
Java Editor

Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>

Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Sarika Sinha <sarika.sinha@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Refactoring of internal Eclipse JDT UI components.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support complex structural rewrites, including conditional branching (if-else if-else), loop introduction (while loop), and multiple conditional assignments to the same variable, which is beyond current expression-level or statement-level pattern replacement.
summary: This commit refactors the internal `RenameLinkedMode.java` to support renaming Java modules. It involves significant structural changes, including transforming an `if` block into an `if-else if-else` structure, introducing a `while` loop, and performing conditional assignments based on complex logic. These changes are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based code transformations.

commitMessage:
Bug 578693 - Move finders to jdt.core.manipulation

This allows for reuse in jdt.ls.

Signed-off-by: Odin Dahlström <zerodind@gmail.com>
Change-Id: Ie538e74a263e0762fc0596d302caf73f3c51fb33
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190713
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Roland Grunberg <rgrunber@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This commit performs an architectural refactoring by moving internal classes and their associated resources (NLS messages) between packages/modules, which is outside the scope of pattern-based DSL rules.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL cannot manage file/package restructuring, changes in resource bundles (.properties files), or the removal/addition of entire classes. While FQN replacement is supported, the overarching task of moving classes between modules and managing related resource files falls outside the DSL's capabilities.
summary: This commit moves internal JDT 'finder' classes and their associated NLS messages from `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.search` to `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.core.manipulation.search`. This is an architectural refactoring involving package moves and resource file changes, which cannot be expressed or automated by the TriggerPattern DSL. Although the DSL can handle FQN replacements, the overall scope of this change makes it a RED transformation.

commitMessage:
Bug 578490 - NPE in CPListElement.toString()

Avoid throwing a NPE in CPListElement.toString() if the classpath entry
could not be created (e.g. due to entry kind set to -1).

Change-Id: Ie6092f5dde61122bfd264de98c7486bec17b7b20
Signed-off-by: Simeon Andreev <simeon.danailov.andreev@gmail.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190208
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change fixes a specific bug within an internal JDT UI component related to build paths.
languageChangeNeeded: The transformation involves extracting an expression into a new local variable and then introducing a multi-line 'if-else' control flow block based on the variable's nullity. The current TriggerPattern DSL does not support structural rewrites of this complexity, such as adding new local variable declarations or inserting 'if-else' statements in the replacement.
summary: This commit fixes a NullPointerException in `CPListElement.toString()` by adding an explicit null check for the `IClasspathEntry` before calling `toString()`. The fix introduces a new local variable and an `if-else` block to handle the null case. This is a structural rewrite (adding a variable, adding control flow) that is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level and simple statement replacements. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 575864 - CoreASTProvider.getAST() wakes up sleeping reconciler

when getAST() waits for reconciler result.

Reproduce: Reduces getAST() time during
org.eclipse.jdt.text.tests.contentassist.CodeCompletionTest

Change-Id: I29488dcdc91a661fd14206961d08cada6b3b6ac4
Signed-off-by: Joerg Kubitz <jkubitz-eclipse@gmx.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/186868
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The changes are deeply integrated into the internal JDT UI reconciliation process, modifying the lifecycle and communication between components.
languageChangeNeeded: This commit introduces a new default method to an interface, modifies several method signatures (which DSL cannot do directly without using MethodDeclaration patterns for annotations), and relocates and modifies existing statements (moving a CoreASTProvider call from one method to a newly introduced one, and adding an argument to it). The DSL does not support adding new method declarations to interfaces nor the complex relocation and modification of existing statements within method bodies.
summary: This commit introduces a new `aboutToWork` lifecycle method (as a Java 8+ default method) in `IJavaReconcilingListener` and updates several internal JDT UI classes to implement and use it. Specifically, an existing `CoreASTProvider` method call is moved from `aboutToBeReconciled()` to the new `aboutToWork()` method, and its signature is changed from one argument to two (adding a method reference `javaReconciler::signalWaitForFinish`). The introduction of a new default method in an interface and the complex relocation and modification of a statement across different method bodies are structural changes that are not directly expressible by the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this commit is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 578242 - [18] DOM AST support for JEP 413

Change-Id: I78633d40ed24890a536c9b94831ad695db4c7a89
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190933
categoryReason: The change is an internal refactoring within JDT UI's Javadoc processing code, related to AST support.
summary: The commit replaces calls to `$tagProp.getValue()` with `$tagProp.getStringValue()` on instances of `JavadocContentAccess2.TagProperty` within `JavadocContentAccess2.java`. This change is an internal JDT UI refactoring to support JEP 413 (DOM AST support for Javadoc tags with properties) for Java 18 and higher. It is a direct 1:1 method call replacement, enhancing internal API correctness and is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The rule is guarded by an `instanceof` check for the specific internal type and a `sourceVersionGE(18)` check to align with the JEP's introduction.

commitMessage:
Bug 578819 - Refactor rename no longer changes file without inputting
Enter

There are two different methods LinkedNodeFinder.findByNode(root,
nameNode) and the difference is in the argument type - Name vs
SimpleName. Previous refactoring changed "SimpleName nameNode" to "Name
nameNode" and that caused different implementation to be used.

Change-Id: I526c87c24e2e431319868508ac7eb14657f26773
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/190938
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: The fix addresses an internal bug in JDT's linked mode refactoring, not a general code pattern for user applications.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for refactoring local variable declarations (type, initializer, scope) conditionally within if/else blocks, and rearranging statement order. The DSL does not support such complex structural and scope-dependent transformations.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in `RenameLinkedMode.java` related to incorrect overload resolution for `LinkedNodeFinder.findByNode()`. The fix involves introducing type-specific local variable declarations and repositioning assignments within an `if-else` structure to ensure the correct `findByNode` overload is invoked based on whether the selected node is a `Name` or `SimpleName`. This is a complex structural refactoring of local variable scope, type-dependent initialization, and statement reordering, which is beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.

categoryReason: The change improves the visual quality of UI elements (icons) on high-resolution displays.
summary: This commit addresses Bug 497947 by modifying an internal JDT UI method call to `ImagesOnFileSystemRegistry.getImageURL(IJavaElement)`. It adds a `zoom` argument (with value `200`) to request a high-resolution version of icons for Javadoc hovers, improving display quality on Retina screens. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement with an added literal argument, which is fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. Other changes in the commit, such as introducing new method overloads and modifying their bodies, are structural and not directly implementable via expression-based DSL rules.

commitMessage:
Bug 578919 - [18][javadoc] provide support for reporting invalid
snippets in javadoc

Change-Id: Ieaa0c9da32374dd32b310d6b731a95b066595c75
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191104
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need capabilities for: 
- Modifying method signatures (changing parameter types and count).
- Inserting arbitrary control flow (new 'if-else' blocks).
- Adding new private helper methods.
- Replacing a loop over a generic list with a loop over a raw list (type parameter change in iteration).
summary: This commit introduces structural changes to JDT's internal Javadoc rendering logic to support new snippet features and report invalid snippets. The changes involve modifying a method signature, introducing new control flow, and adding an internal helper method, all of which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based transformations.

commitMessage:
Bug 578794 - [18] Merge from master to BETA_JAVA18 Periodically

merge post 4.23 RC1
categoryReason: The change modifies internal JDT UI API usage.
summary: This commit is a merge with multiple internal JDT refactorings. While many changes are structural (variable reordering, method overloading) and not expressible by the DSL, one specific pattern is a direct, automatable method call replacement. It adds a literal '200' argument to an internal 'getImageURL' method call within 'JavadocHover.java', which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. The rule is categorized as JDT Internal Refactoring and improves internal API consistency.

commitMessage:
Bug 578041 - [18][javadoc] provide support for regions in @snippet in
@highlight, @replace, or @link tags

Change-Id: If11d684faa9aeec4a36ba62efb0b1c182e95539e
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191323
categoryReason: This commit extends Javadoc processing functionality, a new area not covered by existing categories.
languageChangeNeeded: This commit introduces complex new Java logic including multiple new methods, loops, intricate conditional structures, and custom AST traversal/manipulation logic (e.g., `getRegionsBeforeASTNode`, `getRegionsAfterASTNode`, `getDummyJavadocRegionString`). The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for pattern matching and transformation of existing code, not for generating or rewriting complex control flow or introducing new methods with custom programmatic logic. Implementing these changes would require significant extensions to the DSL's capabilities, beyond simple expression replacement or method signature modification.
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT UI Javadoc content access to support region-based markup within `@snippet` tags. It introduces several new helper methods (`getTextElementString`, `getJavaDocRegionString`, `getDummyJavadocRegionString`, `getRegionsBeforeASTNode`, `getRegionsAfterASTNode`) and significantly rewrites existing ones. These changes involve complex programmatic logic including loops, conditional statements, and intricate AST node manipulation which are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 578040 - [18][javadoc] provide support for @link markup in @snippet

Change-Id: I0d0e5ac0697e6f7e91087b183f5e8343a1abde1a
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191359
categoryReason: Builds upon the same Javadoc processing functionality introduced in the previous commit.
languageChangeNeeded: This commit introduces new complex Java logic, including the `handleSnippetLink` and `getLinkRef` methods, which involve regular expression parsing (`Pattern.compile`, `Matcher`), `StringBuilder` manipulation, multiple `instanceof` checks, conditional branching (`if-else if-else` structures, `while` loops), and custom URI generation with exception handling. The TriggerPattern DSL is not capable of generating such intricate programmatic logic or handling runtime aspects like regex parsing and exception flows. While the addition of `else if (TagElement.TAG_LINK.equals(name))` to existing conditional chains is a direct structural modification, the implementation of the called methods is too complex for the DSL.
summary: This commit enhances Javadoc processing to support `@link` markup within `@snippet` tags. It introduces complex new methods like `handleSnippetLink` (which involves regex matching, string building, and conditional replacements) and `getLinkRef` (which handles Java element link generation with various AST node types and URI construction). These changes are highly programmatic, involving intricate control flow, data structures, and external API calls (e.g., `Pattern`, `URI`), which cannot be expressed or generated by the declarative TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, this is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 578788 - Localization popup complains about missing key

- modify NLSHintHelper.getAccessorClassReference() to check if a
  static String reference is from a class without a bundle resource
  in which case return null
- modify NLSStringHover.internalGetHoverInfo() to verify if the
  class referenced has NLS as superclass in which case treat the
  name as key, otherwise look at its value to be the key

Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit fixes an internal bug in JDT's NLS hover logic by introducing complex conditional checks and AST traversal to correctly identify NLS keys.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for inserting new `if` blocks, `try-catch` blocks, and arbitrary programmatic AST traversal/binding resolution logic within replacements. The current DSL is limited to expression and declaration rewrites, not complex control flow insertion.
summary: The commit fixes a bug in JDT's internal NLSStringHover by introducing complex conditional logic and AST traversal to more accurately determine NLS keys. This involves inserting a new `try-catch` block, modifying an existing `else if` condition, and wrapping subsequent code in a new `if` block. These are structural changes and involve programmatic AST binding resolution that cannot be expressed with the current TriggerPattern DSL. Hence, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 578909 - [cleanup] Unboxing cleanup causes compile error

- fix UnboxingCleanUp MethodInvocation visitor to ignore calls
  that are cast
- add to CleanUpTest1d5.testDoNotUseUnboxing test

Change-Id: Idcedc70a4e532bfd75e8e38a0e5993ba96c6758f
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191206
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit explicitly states it's a fix for an existing cleanup component.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL would need extensions to express complex AST traversal and conditional control flow (like 'while' loops and 'if/else' statements to skip processing) within pattern matching or a new mechanism to modify the internal logic of an existing Java-based cleanup.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the existing `UnboxingCleanUp` (a Java-based cleanup plugin) to prevent it from causing compile errors. The fix adds internal logic to the `MethodInvocation` visitor to ignore unboxing calls when the result of the invocation is immediately cast. This involves adding a 'while' loop to skip 'ParenthesizedExpression' nodes and then an 'if' condition to check for 'CastExpression' before returning, effectively preventing the cleanup from modifying such code. This is a modification to the internal control flow and decision-making of a Java-based cleanup, rather than a new code transformation pattern. Therefore, it is not expressible in the current TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on declarative `pattern => replacement` rules.

commitMessage:
Bug 578905: Don't add "transient" modifier to local var

Change-Id: I7f39a7c5cefa9268b1442a07fc852c71fce8d81e
Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191137
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change modifies the internal logic of an existing JDT cleanup to prevent an incorrect code transformation, rather than defining a new transformation pattern for user code.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL cannot modify conditional logic within existing Java-based cleanup implementations. This change involves adding a new condition to an 'if' statement within a Java class.
summary: The commit fixes Bug 578905 by modifying the internal logic of `SingleUsedFieldCleanUp` to prevent the incorrect addition of the 'transient' modifier to local variables. This is a bug fix within an existing Java-based cleanup, not a new transformation pattern for user code. The TriggerPattern DSL cannot be used to modify conditional logic within existing Java class implementations, thus classifying this as a RED transformation.

categoryReason: The commit fixes a parsing crash in an existing JDT cleanup, rather than defining a new code transformation pattern for user code.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support modifying the internal control flow of existing cleanup logic (e.g., adding if statements or return statements to visitor methods). This is a structural change to the cleanup's own implementation.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the `ReduceIndentationCleanUp` by adding a conditional check to its `IfStatement` visitor. This prevents parsing crashes in specific unbracketed `if-else` scenarios. This is a fix to the internal logic of an existing Java-based cleanup and is not expressible as a pattern-based TriggerPattern DSL rule as the DSL cannot modify the control flow of cleanup implementations.

categoryReason: The commit fixes a bug in the `StaticInnerClassCleanUp` plugin. The fix involves complex AST manipulation (dynamically reconstructing qualified names and replacing type nodes, clearing properties) and a custom AST visitor, which are beyond the capabilities of the current TriggerPattern DSL for pattern-based replacements.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL does not support defining custom AST visitors for pre-analysis. The required AST transformation involves dynamically constructing complex `QualifiedName` and `Type` nodes based on type bindings and then replacing existing nodes, which is beyond direct pattern-based replacement. It would require programmatic fix functions with extensive AST API access.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the 'Make inner classes static' cleanup. It ensures that instantiations like `this.new Inner()` are correctly transformed to `new Inner()` when the inner class is made static. This involves adding a custom AST visitor to collect relevant `ClassInstanceCreation` nodes and performing complex AST rewrites, including dynamic reconstruction of qualified names and removal of the outer instance expression. This transformation is too complex for the current TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 561484: Add cleanup to remove unused parameters of private methods

Change-Id: I9a47970b2afe0e18b4d4a39c90b128d7ec2eba9b
Signed-off-by: Thomas Mäder <tmader@redhat.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191020
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit adds a new type of code cleanup: removing unused parameters from private methods. This requires complex usage analysis (determining if a parameter is truly unused) and structural modifications to method declarations, which are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently lacks the ability to perform comprehensive usage analysis of method parameters within a method body. It also lacks advanced features for modifying method signatures (removing parameters) and ensuring that call sites are correctly handled, potentially requiring a custom `@FixFunction` with full AST API access and binding resolution.
summary: This commit introduces a new cleanup to remove unused parameters of private methods. This is a complex transformation that requires global usage analysis of method parameters to determine if they are truly unused, and then a structural rewrite of method declarations. Such analysis and multi-node structural modifications are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 578928 - [18][javadoc] provide support for properties for @snippet

Change-Id: Id7a8472d2f169504d7cdcb0178666201952dca91
Signed-off-by: Vikas Chandra <Vikas.Chandra@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191806
Tested-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Internal JDT UI refactoring related to Javadoc rendering.
languageChangeNeeded: Requires support for inserting multi-statement blocks, local variable declarations, and `if-else` control flow in replacements.
summary: This commit modifies internal JDT UI Javadoc rendering logic to support `@snippet` properties by replacing a single `fBuf.append()` call with a conditional block that dynamically generates HTML `<code>` tags with optional `id` attributes. This involves inserting a local variable declaration and an `if-else` statement, which is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL for replacements. Hence, it's classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 579253 - [18][javadoc] support identifiers as values in @snippet for
@highlight @link and @replace

Change-Id: I8ec7a0102e2b4bb485ca955b755550e3ebf483d1
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191831
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: Internal JDT UI refactoring related to Javadoc snippet processing.
summary: This commit removes the internal `stripQuotes` helper method and all its call sites, simplifying `stripQuotes($arg)` to `$arg`. This is because Javadoc `@snippet` values (for `@highlight`, `@link`, `@replace`) now directly support identifiers, removing the need for quoting. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement, fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The rule will be added to `jdt-internal-refactoring.sandbox-hint` and applies to Java 18+ contexts.

summary: The commit fixes typos in a .properties file. This is a resource file modification and does not involve Java code transformation, hence it's not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Bug 578820 - Use new FileLocator.getBundleFileLocation(Bundle)

Change-Id: I676fb759d8c9f7c690c1d9f1820fed5cbbb5183d
Signed-off-by: Hannes Wellmann <wellmann.hannes1@gmx.net>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/191878
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change involves migrating from an older Eclipse API to a newer one, specifically related to bundle file location.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support multi-statement transformations, including introducing new local variable declarations, conditional statements (`if`), and optional unwrapping (`.get()`). Currently, it is limited to expression-level replacements.
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT UI code to use `FileLocator.getBundleFileLocation(Bundle)` which returns an `Optional<File>`, replacing the older `FileLocator.getBundleFile(Bundle)`. This requires introducing a new local variable, handling the `Optional` with an `isEmpty()` check and a subsequent `get()` call, and removing a `try-catch` block. This is a multi-statement, structural transformation that is too complex for the current TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level replacements. Hence, it is classified as RED.

categoryReason: The change modifies an internal method call within the JDT's own cleanup implementation to fix a bug, aligning with internal API usage.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in the `ReduceIndentationCleanUp` by replacing an internal method call chain. Specifically, `ASTNodes.getUnparenthesedExpression(ASTNodeFactory.negate(ast, rewrite, visited.getExpression(), true))` is replaced with `ASTNodeFactory.negateAndUnwrap(ast, rewrite, visited.getExpression(), true, true)`. This is a direct 1:1 method call replacement, including its arguments, which is fully supported by the TriggerPattern DSL. It improves the correctness of an existing JDT cleanup and is a strong candidate for a GREEN rule to modernize internal JDT API usage.

commitMessage:
Bug 579331 - Remove unused code in BuildPathsBlock

Change-Id: If5e6777c51ddef05eb5690154e1d1735f714db90
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192071
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Lars Vogel <Lars.Vogel@vogella.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The commit's primary action is to remove a local variable and its assignments because it is no longer used, which directly falls under dead code elimination.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs a robust mechanism for inter-statement/control-flow analysis or a built-in guard like `isUnused($var)` to safely identify and remove unused local variable declarations and their corresponding assignments. Simply matching the declaration pattern without usage analysis would be unsafe and lead to incorrect removals.
summary: The commit removes an unused local variable (`entries`) and its assignment within a loop, along with a commented-out code block where it was previously used. While removing unused local variables is a valuable code improvement, the TriggerPattern DSL lacks the necessary flow analysis capabilities (e.g., an `isUnused($var)` guard) to safely and generically identify and remove such variables without being overly aggressive. Therefore, this transformation is classified as RED. Eclipse JDT already has a Java-based cleanup for this functionality. The removal of commented-out code is not a code transformation pattern for the DSL.

commitMessage:
Bug 579374 - Use Charset.defaultCharset() for file encoding

Related to Bug 571823 - update usage of some systemproperties

Change-Id: I85c6825d3e6ae7d78275781b939cb92b03fe99f0
Signed-off-by: Carsten Hammer <carsten.hammer@t-online.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/178796
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: Modernizing usage of System.getProperty to a more explicit and robust Charset API.
summary: This commit modernizes the way the default file encoding is retrieved by replacing `System.getProperty("file.encoding")` with `java.nio.charset.Charset.defaultCharset().displayName()`. This is a direct 1:1 expression replacement that improves robustness and clarity, as `System.getProperty` can be problematic. The `Charset.defaultCharset()` and `displayName()` APIs are available since Java 5, which is before the Java 8 baseline, so no `sourceVersionGE` guard is needed. This is a GREEN transformation, suitable for the `misc.sandbox-hint` file, similar to how `System.getProperty("line.separator")` is replaced by `System.lineSeparator()`.

commitMessage:
Bug 567666 - [quick assist] Create an extension from an interface

- Add new proposal that opens up the New Interface Wizard extending
selected interface
- Add testcase
Change-Id: Ie4fd209379f062b16891a4706160723dab684e23
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/189677
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit introduces a new quick assist feature in JDT UI, which involves adding new methods and classes, and modifying control flow. The TriggerPattern DSL is for transforming existing code, not for defining new IDE features or structural additions of this complexity.
summary: This commit adds a new quick assist functionality to JDT UI, enabling users to create new interface implementations from existing interfaces. This involves the creation of a new Java class (`NewInterfaceImplementationProposal`) and a new private method in `QuickAssistProcessor.java`, along with modifications to existing control flow to integrate this new feature. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for pattern-based code transformations, not for defining new IDE features, adding structural elements like new classes or methods, or complex control flow logic for UI interaction. Therefore, this commit is not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Bug 577723 - [18] Remove JCP disclaimers from code, UI and marketplace

Change-Id: Id69ca07f290d7c7887d03f597c8d4e10307adb13
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192174
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
irrelevantReason: The primary changes involve removing multi-line Javadoc-like block comments and commenting out blocks of Java code, neither of which is directly supported by the TriggerPattern DSL for code transformation. The DSL can replace string literals within Javadoc, but not the entire block comment structure itself. Changes in `.properties` files are also outside the scope of the Java code transformation DSL.
summary: This commit removes JCP disclaimers from various Java source files and a properties file, and comments out a block of Java code. The removal of multi-line block comments (including Javadoc) is not directly supported as a pattern in the TriggerPattern DSL for transformation, as comments are typically stripped during parsing before rules are applied. Similarly, commenting out code and modifying `.properties` files are outside the scope of the DSL's current capabilities, which focus on transforming Java code expressions and statements. Therefore, this commit is not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:
Bug 579393 - Simplify lambda expression creates invalid statement

- add check for captured type in LambdaExpressionAndMethodRefCleanUp
  ReplaceLambdaOperation.copyType() method
- add new test in CleanUpTest1d8

Change-Id: Ib55c0a83ccf7790ea69b7b05efbe435d2dac7328
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/192200
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: The change is an internal fix within JDT's own cleanup logic, addressing a bug in type handling for lambda expressions.
languageChangeNeeded: The TriggerPattern DSL needs support for complex conditional statements or blocks in the replacement part of a rule (e.g., nested `if-else` blocks or advanced ternary-like structures that can contain temporary variable derivations and multiple return paths). The current DSL supports only a single expression or a sequence of simple statements in the replacement.
summary: This commit fixes a bug in JDT's internal `LambdaExpressionAndMethodRefCleanUp` by refining how `ITypeBinding`s for captured types are processed during lambda simplification. It replaces a simple assignment of an erased type with a complex nested conditional logic that first checks if the type binding is a capture and, if so, uses its type bounds if available, otherwise falls back to erasure. This transformation introduces control flow (`if-else` blocks) into the assignment's right-hand side, which is not supported by the current TriggerPattern DSL's expression-based replacement mechanism. Thus, it is classified as RED.

commitMessage:
Bug 99622 Rename method misses ambiguously overridden method

RippleMethodFinder2 now has a MultiMap for the fTypeToMethod field
because with Generics it is possible to implement more than one method
in a super interface

Change-Id: I187127fae2dc2d6439a795bef915109875ca1eca
Signed-off-by: Nikolay Metchev <nikolaymetchev@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/70037
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: Refactoring of internal Eclipse JDT UI component usage.
summary: This commit addresses a bug in method renaming. The primary architectural change involves the deletion of `RenameMethodUserInterfaceStarter.java` and a significant rewrite of node finding/sorting logic in `RenameLinkedMode.java` to support generic method overriding. These structural and complex logic changes are beyond the current DSL capabilities. However, a simpler, automatable pattern is identified in `RenameUserInterfaceManager.java`, where `RenameMethodUserInterfaceStarter.class` is replaced by `RenameUserInterfaceStarter.class` for two specific `put` method calls. This is a direct 1:1 type reference replacement in method arguments, fully expressible as a GREEN TriggerPattern DSL rule. The rule targets an internal JDT API modernization and does not rely on new Java language features beyond the baseline Java 8.

While technically a transformation, generating rules for reverts of specific internal changes is generally not useful for broad-purpose DSL mining.
categoryReason: Reverting a previous internal refactoring.
summary: This commit is a revert of parts of Commit 0 (c8ab3558cf8d482557e53d04f26e2713535580ed). It restores the `RenameMethodUserInterfaceStarter.java` class and reverts the `RenameUserInterfaceManager.java` file to use `RenameMethodUserInterfaceStarter.class` again for method refactoring processors. Since this is reversing a specific internal change, it's not a new, generalizable transformation pattern suitable for DSL mining. The changes are the inverse of the pattern identified in Commit 0.

commitMessage:
Bug 540546 - Use text font for source text

The default font is not monospace which makes the bytecode less readable
than with the text font. Also react on changes of the text font.

Change-Id: I45a666a283b17527969de2e1217b7a55fda7adcc
Signed-off-by: Karsten Thoms <karsten.thoms@karakun.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/+/131570
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: Enhancement to UI element rendering, but involves structural code insertion.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs support for inserting arbitrary statements or control flow blocks into existing method bodies, not just replacing expressions or full method declarations.
summary: This commit fixes Bug 540546 by ensuring bytecode source text uses a monospace font (text font) for better readability. The changes involve adding new statements (`fNoSourceTextWidget.setFont(JFaceResources.getTextFont());`) within an existing `if` block and inside a method (`createNoSourceTextWidget`). The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for expression replacements and method declaration transformations, not for inserting new `if` blocks or arbitrary statements within existing method bodies. Therefore, this pattern is classified as RED, as it requires deeper structural rewrite capabilities beyond the current DSL scope.
